A job message must be a struct - messages are plain JSON structs. A
//fabrik:job whose last parameter is a named slice (or scalar/map) is
rejected at generation, matching the runtime Register check.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli => CLIDIR

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs => JOBSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- shared/shared.go --
package shared

import (
	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"
)

//fabrik:provider
func NewJobStore() (jobs.Store, error) { return jobs.NewMemoryStore(), nil }

//fabrik:cli:command
func Boot(ctx cli.Context, mgr *jobs.Manager) error { return nil }
-- mail/mail.go --
package mail

import "context"

type Recipients []string

//fabrik:job
func SendAll(ctx context.Context, r Recipients) error { return nil }
-- want/diags --
error: mail/mail.go:7:1: handler SendAll: the message parameter must be a struct
  help: messages are plain JSON structs; a scalar, slice, or map is not a message type
